# CEDMr ‚Äî R CMD check Fixes
# ============================================================

## Summary of all errors / warnings / notes and their fixes

| # | Severity | Message | Root cause | Fix file |
|---|----------|---------|------------|----------|
| 1 | **ERROR** | Infinite recursion in `print.cedm_sensitivity` | `x[, cols]` keeps class `cedm_sensitivity` ‚Üí `print()` recurses | `cedm_sensitivity_fix.R` |
| 2 | NOTE | `no visible binding for global variable '.data'` etc. | Bare names in `aes()` / dplyr not declared | `globals.R` |
| 3 | NOTE | `no visible global function definition for ':='` and `complete.cases` | Missing `@importFrom` declarations | `roxygen_imports.R` |
| 4 | **WARNING** | Undocumented: `cedm_full_pipeline`, `cedm_rewb`, `cedm_trajectory` | Stray `#' @importFrom magrittr %>%` above function roxygen blocks swallows the docs | `doc_stubs.R` + source edits |
| 5 | NOTE | `License stub is invalid DCF` | LICENSE file is a template placeholder | Edit DESCRIPTION |
| 6 | NOTE | `Imports not imported from: 'cluster' 'konfound' 'methods'` | Packages listed in Imports but never called | Edit DESCRIPTION |

---

## Step-by-step instructions

### Step 1 ‚Äî Copy the three new R files into your package

Copy from this folder into `R/`:

```
R/globals.R
R/roxygen_imports.R
R/cedm_sensitivity_fix.R   (replaces/updates the print method only)
```

> **Important:** In your original `cedm_sensitivity.R`, delete (or comment out)
> the existing `print.cedm_sensitivity` function and replace it with the one in
> `cedm_sensitivity_fix.R`.

---

### Step 2 ‚Äî Fix the stray `@importFrom` at the top of two source files

**Why this matters:**  
`cedm_trajectory.R` and `cedm_pipeline.R` both begin with:

```r
#' @importFrom magrittr %>%
#' Longitudinal Health Trajectory Clustering  ‚Üê roxygen2 sees this as
#'                                               the title for NULL, not
#'                                               for the function below
```

roxygen2 attaches the `@importFrom` to a blank `NULL` topic and then has
**no roxygen block** left for `cedm_trajectory` / `cedm_full_pipeline`,
so those functions appear undocumented.

**Fix:** Delete line 1 (`#' @importFrom magrittr %>%`) from both files.
The `%>%` import is now centralised in `roxygen_imports.R`.

---

### Step 3 ‚Äî Edit `DESCRIPTION`

```
# Change License: (if your LICENSE file is a stub)
License: MIT

# Remove unused Imports:
Imports:
    dplyr,
    ggplot2,
    lme4,
    magrittr,
    purrr,
    rlang,
    rms,
    stats,
    tidyr
```

If you actually use `cluster` (hierarchical clustering),
keep it in Imports **and** add `#' @importFrom cluster agnes`
(or whatever function you call) to `roxygen_imports.R`.

---

### Step 4 ‚Äî Regenerate the NAMESPACE and docs

```r
devtools::document()   # re-runs roxygen2
devtools::check()      # should now be clean
```

---

## What caused the infinite recursion (detailed)

```r
# BEFORE (broken):
print.cedm_sensitivity <- function(x, ...) {
  ...
  print(x[, display_cols], row.names = FALSE)
  # x[, display_cols] returns an object of class c("cedm_sensitivity","data.frame")
  # so R dispatches back to print.cedm_sensitivity ‚Üí infinite loop
}

# AFTER (fixed):
print.cedm_sensitivity <- function(x, ...) {
  ...
  x_plain <- x[, display_cols, drop = FALSE]
  class(x_plain) <- "data.frame"   # strip custom class first
  print(x_plain, row.names = FALSE) # now dispatches to print.data.frame
}
```

---

## After all fixes, expected `devtools::check()` output

```
0 errors ‚úì | 0 warnings ‚úì | 0 notes ‚úì
```
